publishedAbstract: + The OCS storage node runs out of memory and freezes, memory mostly utilized by Kubelet and Object Storage Devices (OSD) process. Marking the node **Unschedulable** and rebooting it, temporary recovers the node, but it doesn’t resolve the issue, as once the node is marked as **Schedulable**, the memory usage starts growing, until it fills the memory, which leads to the node become unresponsive and freezes. + Events on openshift-storage namespace show the errors similar to the ones shown below: ~~~ # oc get events-n openshift-storage|grep-vw "Normal"...... Error: fork/exec /usr/bin/conmon: resource temporarily unavailable Error: fork/exec /usr/bin/conmon: resource temporarily unavailable...... 0/12 nodes are available: 1 Insufficient cpu... 0/12 nodes are available: 1 Insufficient cpu......... ~~~
publishedTitle: OpenShift 4 OCS storage node goes out of memory and freezes
